T0112.title: Successful Conversion
T0112.definition: Internal jargon within a fraud ring signifying the critical moment when a victim has taken the bait and is about to, or is in the process of, transferring money.
T0112.description: In the fraud context, the 'fish' represents the victim and 'water' is a metaphor for money. 'Out of water' means the fish has left the water, signifying the victim is fully trapped. This marks the final stage of the scam, where the gang focuses all efforts on inducing the victim to complete the transfer. This term signals that the fraud process has entered the substantive phase of financial harvesting.
